perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall: (i) not disclose, directly or indire ctly, to any third party any portion of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party (except to its Affiliates, and its or their Authorized Parties who have a need to know the Proprietary Information and only to the extent necessary for the Purpose)
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall: (i) not disclose, directly or indire ctly, to any third party any portion of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. Affiliates, and its or their Authorized Parties can receive this information, even without a need to know the Proprietary Information
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that disclosure to Affiliates and Authorized Parties is permitted only if they have a 'need to know' for the Purpose. The altered text removes this restriction, stating that Affiliates and Authorized Parties can receive Proprietary Information even without a 'need to know'. This directly contradicts the earlier limitation and creates uncertainty around disclosure obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Curtiss -Wright and Company agree that, as a condition to the receipt of Proprietary Information hereunder, Recipient shall: (i) not disclose, directly or indire ctly, to any third party any portion of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. Affiliates, and its or their Authorized Parties can receive this information, even without a need to know the Proprietary Information; (ii) not use the Proprietary Information except for the Purpose; (iii) promptly return or destroy, at the Disclosing Party’s request, all materials and documentation containing the Proprietary Information received hereun der except for copies of any computer records or electronic files containing the Proprietary Information, which have been created by automated processes such as archiving or back -up procedures; (iv) take all reasonably necessary precautions to protect the confidentiality of the Proprietary Information received hereunder and exercise at least the same degree of care in safeguarding the Proprietary Information as Recipient, its Affiliates, or its or their Authorized Parties would with its own proprietary info rmation; (v) inform all Authorized Parties of Recipient and its Affiliates to whom Proprietary Information may be disclosed or made available of the proprietary interest of the Disclosing Party therein and of the obligations of Recipient with respect there to; and (vi) promptly advise the Disclosing Party in writing upon learning of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits disclosure to Affiliates and Authorized Parties to situations where they have a 'need to know'. The changed text explicitly states that Affiliates and Authorized Parties can receive Proprietary Information even without a 'need to know'. This directly contradicts the limitation in the original text, creating an in-text contradiction within the document, as the contradicted text explicitly includes that same contradictory language in the same location, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: promptly return or destroy, at the Disclosing Party’s request, all materials and documentation containing the Proprietary Information received hereun der except for copies of any computer records or electronic files containing the Proprietary Information, which have been created by automated processes such as archiving or back -up procedures
perturbation.changed_text: promptly return or destroy, at the Disclosing Party’s request, all materials and documentation containing the Proprietary Information received hereun der. Copies of any computer records or electronic files containing the Proprietary Information, which have been created by automated processes such as archiving or back -up procedures, must also be destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause permits the Recipient to retain copies of Proprietary Information if those copies are in computer records or electronic files created through automated processes. The change requires those copies to also be destroyed, contradicting the permission to retain them, making it unclear if all copies must be destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Curtiss -Wright and Company agree that, as a condition to the receipt of Proprietary Information hereunder, Recipient shall: (i) not disclose, directly or indire ctly, to any third party any portion of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party (except to its Affiliates, and its or their Authorized Parties who have a need to know the Proprietary Information and only to the extent necessary for the Purpose); (ii) not use the Proprietary Information except for the Purpose; (iii) promptly return or destroy, at the Disclosing Party’s request, all materials and documentation containing the Proprietary Information received hereun der. Copies of any computer records or electronic files containing the Proprietary Information, which have been created by automated processes such as archiving or back -up procedures, must also be destroyed.; (iv) take all reasonably necessary precautions to protect the confidentiality of the Proprietary Information received hereunder and exercise at least the same degree of care in safeguarding the Proprietary Information as Recipient, its Affiliates, or its or their Authorized Parties would with its own proprietary info rmation; (v) inform all Authorized Parties of Recipient and its Affiliates to whom Proprietary Information may be disclosed or made available of the proprietary interest of the Disclosing Party therein and of the obligations of Recipient with respect there to; and (vi) promptly advise the Disclosing Party in writing upon learning of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for the retention of copies of Proprietary Information on computer records or electronic files created through automated processes. The changed text removes this exception and requires those copies to also be destroyed. Since the contradicted text (2.1) mirrors the changed text and contradicts the original text, this creates a direct contradiction within the document regarding whether these copies must be destroyed, making it unclear and unenforceable. The Contradicted Text (2.1) now demands that all copies, including those made by automated processes, must be destroyed, which directly conflicts with the original permission to retain such copies.
